meaning: Navigate straight to a specified fix, waypoint, or aerodrome without following the previously cleared route.
portugueseMeaning: Voar diretamente para um ponto ou fixo.
operationalMeaning: ATC shortens your route when traffic and airspace allow a direct track.
whoUsesIt: Center and Approach controllers; pilots acknowledge and navigate direct.
whenUsed: When traffic permits a shortcut, for re-routing around weather, or to improve flow.
whyUsed: Saves time and fuel while maintaining radar separation from other traffic.

realExamples.text: Proceed direct Galeao, descend flight level one four zero.
realExamples.speaker: Approach

icaoQuestions: When would ATC ask a pilot to fly direct?
What navigation action do you take after 'proceed direct'?
commonMistakes: Starting the turn before confirming the correct fix name.
Saying 'OK direct' instead of reading back the fix.
pronunciationTips: Fly di-RECT — stress the second syllable.
Spell unusual fix names clearly if asked.

compareTerms.note: Vectors are heading assignments; direct is navigation to a fix.
Resume own nav returns routing responsibility to the pilot.

didYouKnow: Direct clearances are common in cruise when IFR traffic is radar-identified.
captainStory: Cruise over the interior, Center offered direct Brasília when the sector was quiet — saved twelve minutes without a single vector.

references.title: ICAO Delta ATC Phraseology — Fly Direct
